name: Al Sears
abstract: Albert Omega Sears (February 21, 1910 – March 23, 1990) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and bandleader, sometimes credited as Big Al Sears. Sears was born in Macomb, Illinois, United States.

works.t: I Ain't Got Nothing But The Blues
Sophisticated Ladies: It Don't Mean a Thing
Black, Brown and Beige: Mauve (The Blues)

concerts.label: Ninth Annual WLIB Festival of Negro Music and Drama

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: And he also had Al Sears in the band... So he sent for me... Sears played baritone, I think... I know Procope and I played alto

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: There's a guy named Al Sears. I think he was a tenor player, and he was with -- Duke for quite a spell... Al Sears and three or four of his musicians came in one night... I heard our riff. They've called it 'Castle Rock' and Al Sears or so and so recorded it

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Juan Tizol) does not mention Al Sears. The interviewer mentions visiting Al Sears, but the interviewee's response ('Oh? I imagine he don't play that much.') is a general comment about Cootie Edwards, not a personal recollection of Al Sears.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Schaap
about_quotes.evidence: I would even trade you one of my rarer records for just one second of that in real time again... Big Al Sears had gone into the record business... And he was wonderful to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Al Sears is mentioned only in the context of being Ben Webster's replacement in the Ellington band before Al Sears, in a story about Steve Tropp's brother Pinky Williams.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Al many years before, having met him and Sy (Melvin) Oliver in Zanesville, Ohio, when I played that town with the Scott band before we went to New-York.
